artifact: WHO Surgical Safety Checklist (Sign In / Time Out / Sign Out)
source: WHO Guidelines for Safe Surgery 2009; Joint Commission NPSG.01.01.01
go_nogo: Any unchecked Sign In item = NO-GO induction
Failed airway assessment = escalate to difficult airway algorithm
Missing antibiotics = HOLD incision until administered
Equipment fail = abort

artifact: FAA Part 91 Before-Takeoff Checklist + Takeoff Briefing
steps: Takeoff briefing: V1/Vr/V2, abort criteria, departure
go_nogo: Mag drop >175 RPM or >50 RPM split = NO-GO
Any red CAS/annunciator = NO-GO
Below V1 = REJECT for any abnormality
Above V1 = CONTINUE + troubleshoot airborne

steps: Logbook entry: date, description, hours, signature, cert#
go_nogo: Missing approved data = NO RTS
Failed ops check = NO RTS
AD non-compliance = aircraft GROUNDED

source: IHI/AHRQ TeamSTEPPS; Joint Commission NPSG.02.03.01
steps: S: name, age, admitting dx, code status
A: vitals/trends, drips, lines/tubes/drains, pending labs
R: anticipated issues, parameters to call provider
go_nogo: Unresolved critical lab/vital = escalate before sign-off
Missing code status = MUST clarify
High-alert drips not double-checked = HOLD handoff

source: AHRQ ESI Implementation Handbook 2020 Ed; ENA endorsed
steps: B: high-risk, confused/lethargic, severe pain/distress → ESI 2
go_nogo: ESI 1 = immediate resus bay
ESI 2 = bed within 10 min, MD notified
Danger-zone vitals at ESI 3 = upgrade to ESI 2

source: OSHA Publication 3071; 29 CFR 1910 Subpart I
go_nogo: Uncontrolled high-severity hazard = STOP WORK
Missing PPE = NO-GO
Near-miss in last 30 days on this task = mandatory re-JHA

source: OSHA 29 CFR 1926.20(b); ANSI/ASSP A10.33
steps: Hazards (fall >6 ft, silica, struck-by, electrical, heat)
Rescue plan if working at height/confined
go_nogo: No competent person on site = NO-GO
Missing permits = STOP WORK
No rescue plan for fall/CSE = NO-GO

source: AWS D1.1/D1.1M:2020 Structural Welding Code — Steel
go_nogo: Any crack = REJECT
Undercut > tolerance + cyclically loaded = REJECT
Porosity > Table 8.1/10.8 = REJECT + repair per Clause 7.25

source: ASME BPVC Sec V Article 4; ASNT SNT-TC-1A (2020); CP-189
steps: Calibrate DAC/TCG, IIW block, temp delta
go_nogo: Cal drift >20% amplitude / 10% sweep = invalidate + rescan
Indication >acceptance (ASME Sec VIII App 12) = REJECT
Loss of back-wall echo in plate = investigate

steps: Identify all energy sources (electrical, stored, capacitive)
Shut down equipment in normal sequence
Test before re-energization + remove locks by applier only
go_nogo: Voltage after isolation = STOP, re-isolate
Missing live-dead-live = NO-GO
Cannot establish ESWC → energized work permit required + qualified-person sign

source: AORN Guidelines for Perioperative Practice 2024
steps: Count at relief of either RN or scrub
Discrepancy → search field, drapes, trash, patient
X-ray if not reconciled before patient leaves OR
go_nogo: Incorrect final count = NO transport until imaging
Retained item suspected = mandatory intra-op imaging
Surgeon may NOT override count discrepancy without documented reason

artifact: DEA Form 222 + Biennial Inventory + CII Perpetual Log
source: 21 CFR 1304/1305/1307; DEA Pharmacist's Manual
steps: CII receipt only via DEA Form 222 / CSOS
Record receipt to perpetual same day
Biennial inventory snapshot (CII exact, CIII-V est <1000)
Theft/loss → DEA Form 106 within 1 business day
go_nogo: Count discrepancy = QUARANTINE, investigate, RPh + PIC sign-off
Significant loss = mandatory Form 106
Missing 222 copy 3 = DEA reportable; cannot dispense until reconciled

source: ACR Appropriateness Criteria; ACR Communication Practice Parameter
steps: Incidentals per ACR White Papers (Fleischner, adrenal, renal, thyroid)
go_nogo: Score 1-3 = procedure usually not appropriate; recommend alternative
Cat 1 actionable = call ordering provider, document time/name
Non-diagnostic exam = REPEAT or alternate modality

artifact: CAP Cancer Protocols / Synoptic Reporting Template
source: College of American Pathologists Cancer Protocols; CoC Standard 6.1
go_nogo: Missing required data element = incomplete, NOT CoC-compliant
Positive/close margin = mandatory comment
Biomarker QC failure = repeat before sign-out

source: 42 CFR 493 Subparts H/K; CMS IQCP; CLSI EP23-A
steps: Risk assessment (specimen, env, reagent, system, personnel)
PT samples handled like patient samples (no inter-lab consult)
go_nogo: QC failure = HOLD patient results until corrected
Two consecutive PT failures = unsuccessful; CMS may suspend analyte
IQCP gap = revert to default CLIA QC (2 levels/day)

source: Codex Alimentarius CXC 1-1969 Rev 2020; 21 CFR 117; 9 CFR 417
go_nogo: Critical limit exceeded = HOLD product, evaluate, document CA
Uncalibrated monitoring device = product since last cal SUSPECT
Failed verification = reassess HACCP plan

steps: Cooking final internals (poultry 165 °F, ground 155 °F, whole muscle 145 °F)
Cooling 135→70 °F in 2 hr, 70→41 °F in next 4 hr
go_nogo: TCS out-of-temp >4 hr = DISCARD
Cooling step failure = discard
Thermometer ±2 °F drift = recalibrate / replace
≥2 consecutive failures = inspector-notifiable

steps: Active air sampling (≥1 m³ Grade A)
go_nogo: Grade A viable >1 CFU = action, investigate batch impact
≥1 colony Grade A glove print = operator requalification
Sustained excursion = batch rejection + media-fill review

source: 21 CFR 211.188/211.192; ICH Q7 §6; EU GMP Vol 4 Ch 4
steps: Material identity + lot + qty vs MBR
In-process parameters in range (pH, DO, temp, agitation, feed)
go_nogo: Unexplained deviation = batch QA HOLD
Out-of-trend yield = investigation before release
Missing signature on critical step = cannot release

source: ASME CSD-1; NBIC NB-23 Part 2; NFPA 85
go_nogo: LWCO failure = TRIP boiler
Safety valve not lifting at set = REMOVE from service
Chemistry out of spec = blowdown + chemist callout before continued ops

source: IEEE 1184/IEEE C2 (NESC); NERC PER-005; OSHA 1910.269
steps: Written switching order prepared + checked + approved
Issue clearance to crew with unique number
Crew works only within clearance scope
Release clearance, remove grounds, restore in reverse
go_nogo: Voltage after isolation = STOP, re-isolate, re-verify
Mismatched switching order step = HALT, escalate to dispatcher
No grounds applied where required = NO work permitted

source: 49 CFR 192 Subpart O; API 1163; NACE SP0102
steps: Receive — confirm tool integrity + data quality
go_nogo: Immediate condition (≥80% wt loss / FPP ≤1.1×MOP) = pressure reduction or shutdown within hours
60/180-day conditions per §192.933
ILI data-quality fail = re-run

source: API RP 76 + RP 14C; IOGP 459; OSHA 1910.119
steps: Permit type (hot / confined / electrical / diving / cold)
Time-bound permit; suspended on alarm / shift change
go_nogo: LEL ≥10% = NO hot work
O2 outside 19.5-23.5% = NO entry
Alarm/ESD/MOC change = permit auto-suspended

steps: Methane test (≥0.1% record; ≥1.0% withdraw non-ess; ≥1.5% withdraw all)
Ventilation — quantity + direction at key points
Record in approved book before miners enter
go_nogo: Methane ≥1.0% in working place = withdraw + de-energize
Missing pre-shift = no miners may enter
Uncorrected hazard = area dangered off until abated

source: 29 CFR 1910.119(i); CCPS PSSR Guidelines; API RP 750
steps: Safety / operating / maintenance / emergency procedures in place
Punch-list A/B/C with B closed pre-startup
go_nogo: Open Cat-A item = NO startup
PHA action unresolved = NO startup
Missing operator training = NO startup
Missing PSSR signature = cannot introduce hydrocarbons

source: 23 CFR 650 Subpart C (2022 NBIS); FHWA SNBI; AASHTO MBE
go_nogo: Rating ≤4 = poor; increased scrutiny
Rating ≤3 = serious, immediate evaluation
Scour Code 0-3 = bridge closed
Capacity < legal load = post or close

source: NFPA 502 2023; FHWA Tunnel OMIE; 23 CFR 650 Subpart E (NTIS)
steps: Ventilation — jet fans, dampers, smoke-control mode
go_nogo: Failed smoke-control test = restriction on HGV/HAZMAT
Non-functional FD comms = repair before reopen
Egress blocked = immediate bore closure

source: ASME A17.1-2022; ASME A17.2 Inspector's Guide; AHJ
steps: Car safety + governor trip (Cat 1 annual / Cat 5 every 5 yr)
Brake test — emergency stop with rated load
Firefighters' Emergency Operation Phase I + II
go_nogo: Failed safety/governor = REMOVE from service
Door interlock bypass = remove from service
FEO Phase I/II failure = code violation, repair
Missing Cert of Inspection = AHJ shutdown

source: NBIC NB-23 Part 2; ASME BPVC Sec I/IV/VI/VII
go_nogo: Pressure-retaining defect (crack, bulge, severe thinning) = ORDER out of service
Safety valve fails to lift at set = remove until repaired
Missing data plate / no NB# = jurisdictional violation

source: ASHRAE Guideline 0-2019; Guideline 1.1-2019; ACG Cx
steps: Systems Manual + Final Cx Report + 10-month warranty
go_nogo: Failed FPT = issue log + retest after correction
Missing pre-functional sign-off = no FPT permitted
Substantial completion withheld until Cx issues resolved or deferral plan accepted

source: FM 4471; FM Global DS 1-29
steps: Deck type + condition vs approved assembly
Insulation type, thickness, attachment per RoofNav
Fastener / adhesive pattern matches wind zone
Flashings, edge metal per ANSI/SPRI ES-1 + FM 1-49
Uplift testing (ASTM E907 / FM 1-52) if required
go_nogo: Assembly deviation = loses FM Approval
Failed uplift test = re-attach
Non-ES-1 edge metal = FM non-compliant for windstorm

source: ASME B30.5 2021; OSHA 1926 Subpart CC; 1910.180
steps: Wire rope per B30.30 (broken wires, kinks, corrosion)
Safety devices — boom angle, swing alarm, level
go_nogo: Hook throat opening >15% / twist >10° = REMOVE
LMI inop = NO lift if required by configuration
≥6 broken wires in one lay = retire rope
Brake failure = TAG OUT

source: 49 CFR 396.11/396.13; FMCSA; CVSA OOS Criteria
steps: Brake — air build + leak test (≤3 psi/min single, ≤4 combo); low-air warning ≥55 psi; parking brake
Coupling — fifth wheel, kingpin, safety devices
go_nogo: Air leak / low-warning fail = OOS
Steer tire <4/32 = OOS
Broken/loose lug = OOS
Cargo securement fail = OOS

source: IMO STCW (Manila); IMO Res A.893(21) Passage Planning; ISM Code
go_nogo: UKC < company min = abort / await tide
Pilot card mismatch w/ conning expectation = stop, clarify
ECDIS non-official mode = NO-GO navigation
Loss of one steering mode in pilotage = manual + Master to bridge

artifact: Pre-Departure / Class I Brake Test + Freight Car Inspection
steps: Class I initial-terminal air brake test (90 psi, 15 psi reduction, leakage ≤5 psi/min)
Wheels — flat spots, shelled tread, cracks, flange wear per 215.103
Safety appliances — handholds, ladders, sill steps
Train make-up — tonnage, DPU, blue flag
go_nogo: Defective wheel (flat ≥2.5", crack) = bad-order
Air brake leak >5 psi/min = correct before departure
Missing safety appliance = car cannot be in train

artifact: Daily Operator's Round Sheet / Process Control Log
source: EPA NPDES (40 CFR 122/136); WEF MOP 11
steps: Aeration — DO ≥2 mg/L, MLSS, F/M, SVI
Effluent — turbidity, TSS, NH3, DO vs permit
go_nogo: Effluent excursion vs NPDES = notify per permit (often 24 hr); 5-day written report
DO crash in aeration = blower up + investigate toxic load
Disinfection failure = divert / increase dose

source: 40 CFR 141 Subpart Q; EPA 816-R-09-013
steps: Acute health risk? (nitrate, fecal/E. coli, ClO2, turbidity TT, outbreak)
Issue PN to public within 24 hr (radio/TV/door/social)
go_nogo: Confirmed E. coli MCL violation = immediate Tier 1 + boil-water advisory
Failure to notify within 24 hr = enforcement action
Alternative water / do-not-drink may be required

source: ANSI/ASSP A10.48-2016; OSHA 1926 Subpart M; NATE CTS
steps: RF awareness — survey, monitor, lock-out tx if needed
Tailboard sign-off by climbers + competent person
go_nogo: Wind >40 mph (or plan limit) = NO climb
RF over MPE = lock-out tx or NO-GO
No rescue plan + rescue-capable personnel = NO climb
Damaged harness = remove from service

source: INPO TQ-303; INPO 06-002 HU Tools; 10 CFR 50 App B; NEI 03-08
steps: Procedure in hand, verified current revision
Defenses — peer check, concurrent verification, 3-way comm
Comms plan w/ control room + supervision
go_nogo: Wrong-rev procedure = STOP
Unresolved questioning-attitude flag = HOLD until SRO concurs
Critical step w/o verification method = NO start
Abnormal indication = enter abnormal/emergency procedure

source: ITIL 4 Practice Guide; ISO/IEC 20000-1; Uptime Institute Tier
go_nogo: No backout plan = REJECT
CAB not approved by start time = NO-GO
Failed validation = invoke backout
Emergency change requires post-hoc ECAB review

source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev 2; SP 800-184; SANS PICERL
steps: Detection + analysis — triage, scope, classify severity
Containment — short + long-term (isolate host, block IOC)
Eradication — remove malware, close vuln, rotate creds
Notifications — legal, comms, regulators (GDPR 72 h, SEC 4-day, HIPAA, state)
go_nogo: Confirmed data exfil = escalate to IC + legal
Containment ineffective = expand isolation scope
Regulatory clock starts at discovery — escalation required

source: PTES; OWASP WSTG v4.2; NIST SP 800-115
go_nogo: Out-of-scope discovery = STOP, notify POC, written approval before continuing
Production-impact exploit (DoS, data destruction) = NO unless explicitly authorized
Emergency contact unresponsive = pause engagement

source: Google SRE Workbook Ch 8-9; PagerDuty IR; FEMA ICS
steps: Assign IC, Comms Lead, Ops Lead
go_nogo: No declared IC = anyone can grab role
Risky mitigation = IC decision; document
Mitigation fails twice = escalate; consider full failover
Data integrity risk = prioritize integrity over availability

source: 31 CFR 1020.220; 31 CFR 1010.230; USA PATRIOT §326; FFIEC Manual
steps: Collect 4 CIP elements (name, DOB, address, ID#/TIN)
Source of funds for higher risk
go_nogo: OFAC hit = block + blocking report ≤10 days
Cannot verify identity = decline account
Suspicious activity = SAR within 30 days
Structuring CTR-avoidance = SAR + CTR

source: PCAOB AS 2110; AS 2401; SAS 145
steps: Inquiries of mgmt, audit committee, internal audit
Presumed risks: revenue recognition + management override
Link risks to planned audit responses
go_nogo: Identified fraud risk = mandatory specific response (unpredictable procedures, journal-entry testing)
Illegal-act suspicion = escalate to audit committee + consider 10A reporting
Cannot rebut revenue-recognition presumption = always significant risk

source: Treasury Circular 230; IRC §6695(g); Form 8867; Pub 4687
steps: Make additional inquiries if info appears incorrect/inconsistent/incomplete
Inform client of consequences of false claims
Determine signing vs non-signing preparer responsibilities
go_nogo: Cannot substantiate eligibility = do NOT claim credit
Reckless/willful failure = $635/return penalty (2025 indexed)
Conflict of interest unresolved = withdraw per §10.29

source: 12 CFR 1026 Reg Z; CFPB TRID; RESPA 12 CFR 1024
steps: LE within 3 business days of application (6 elements)
Intent to proceed before fees beyond credit report
CD delivered ≥3 business days before consummation
go_nogo: Zero-tolerance violation = cure to consumer
CD timing miss = re-issue + restart 3-day clock; NO closing
APR variance >1/8% (reg) or 1/4% (irreg) = redisclose
Missed rescission notice = 3-year rescission window

artifact: Claim Investigation File / Coverage + Damages Workup
source: NAIC Unfair Claims Settlement Practices Model Act (#900); state UCSPA
steps: Acknowledge claim per state deadlines (often 10-15 days)
Verify coverage — policy in force, named insured, perils, endorsements, exclusions
Investigation — recorded statement, scene, photos, police/fire report
Damages — estimates, ALE, medical specials, comparative neg
Reserve setting + decision (pay/deny/defend) within statutory time
go_nogo: Insufficient investigation to deny = bad-faith risk; continue investigation
Coverage clearly excluded with no ambiguity = denial letter + policy language
Unreasonable delay = UCSPA violation

source: GRI 1 Foundation 2021; GRI 2; GRI 3
steps: Apply reporting principles (accuracy, balance, clarity, comparability, completeness, sustainability context, timeliness, verifiability)
GRI 3 — determine material topics via impact-based assessment
Per material topic — management approach + topic-specific (200/300/400)
Reasons for omission, if any, per allowed cases
go_nogo: Cannot justify omission per GRI 1 §3 = 'in accordance' claim fails
Materiality based only on financial impact = non-conformance
Missing content index = non-conformance

source: Guide for the Care + Use of Lab Animals 8th Ed (NRC); PHS Policy IV; 9 CFR Part 2 Subpart C (AWA); OLAW
steps: PI submits protocol with scientific rationale
USDA Pain Category (B/C/D/E) with justification for D/E
DMR or FCR by quorum; semi-annual program + facility review
go_nogo: Any IACUC member calls FCR = full committee review required; no DMR
Deficiencies = approve with mods / withhold
Significant non-compliance = report to OLAW + USDA
Cat E without justification = NOT approvable
